task_completion.description: Open the dive site management interface and locate the target dive site.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Subsurface dive site management window or location panel is open, and the site originally named 'Sund Rock, Hoodsport, WA, USA' is selected or visible.

task_completion.description: Rename the dive site to the specified new name.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The location/site name input field is updated to exactly 'Sund Rock (Hood Canal)'.

task_completion.description: Add the specified text to the dive site's Notes or Description field.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Notes or Description text area for the dive site contains the text 'Marine Preserve'.

task_completion.description: Save the dive logbook to persist the changes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent clicks a 'Save' button, uses the 'File -> Save' menu, or the window title bar loses its 'unsaved changes' indicator (often an asterisk).

task_completion.description: Ensure all associated dives reflect the updated site data.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main dive list or logbook view shows exactly 4 dives with their location updated to 'Sund Rock (Hood Canal)'.

integrity.description: The agent used the Subsurface application GUI to edit the dive site.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Subsurface graphical interface, not a text editor (like nano, vim, or gedit) editing the .ssrf XML file directly.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands to bypass the UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows showing commands like 'sed', 'awk', or python scripts being used to find and replace the dive site name or notes in the file.

integrity.description: The agent saved the file using the application's native functionality.
integrity.visual_evidence: The save action is performed via the Subsurface UI (menu or toolbar), rather than forcefully overwriting the file via external means.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The original dive site name in the file is actually 'Sund Rock, Hoodsport, WA, USA' (the task description loosely refers to it as 'Sund Rock WA'). The target new name must be exactly 'Sund Rock (Hood Canal)'. The expected notes to be added are 'Marine Preserve'. There are exactly 4 dives linked to this location in the dataset, and all 4 should reflect the updated name in the dive list.
